news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final between Spain and holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Monday 15 June: D 0-0 vs Cabo Verde (Atlanta) Sunday 21 June: W 4-0 vs Saudi Arabia (Atlanta) Saturday 27 June: W 1-0 vs Uruguay (Guadalajara) Thursday 2 July (R32): W 3-0 vs Austria (Los Angeles) Monday 6 July (R16): W 1-0 vs Portugal (Dallas) Friday 10 July (QF): W 2-1 vs Belgium (Los Angeles) Tuesday 14 July (SF): W 2-0 vs France (Dallas) Sunday 19 July (F): vs Argentina (New York, 21:00).

news.title: World Cup 2026 - France 4-6 England: Bukayo Saka's hat-trick helps Three Lions finish third in 10-goal thriller
news.lede: Bukayo Saka’s hat-trick helped England to a remarkable 6-4 bronze medal win over France in Miami to achieve their best World Cup finish since 1966. England were incredibly 4-0 up at half-time through Declan Rice's opener, Ezri Konsa's header and Saka's double, as Thomas Tuchel's side took advantage of an uninterested France - but they nearly blew their four-goal lead in the second half. The two-time World Cup winners woke up after half-time to threaten a sensational comeback through Kylian Mbappe's double - which saw him become the World Cup's all-time leading scorer (22 goals) - and substitute Bradley Barcola's strike. Hat-trick hero Saka: I wanted to play more England tournament player ratings: Who impressed and who struggled?

news.title: When is BTS performing at the World Cup? Projected start time for 2026 FIFA halftime show
news.lede: Projected start time for 2026 FIFA halftime show originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here. Argentina and Spain will give the stage up at halftime of the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final. BTS will be a part of a first-of-its-kind halftime show at this World Cup Final.

news.title: Confirmed: Three Barcelona players start in FIFA World Cup final vs Argentina
news.lede: We are just a little more than an hour away from witnessing the final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup as Spain take on Argentina at the Metlife Stadium. Heading into this game, Spain has confirmed its starting eleven that will take on the defending champions, as La Roja look for their second World Cup title. Unsurprisingly, the Spain starting eleven features multiple Barcelona stars, who will have a role to play in helping their nation against Lionel Messi’s Argentina. The Spain starting eleven in the final features three Barcelona players in the form of Pau Cubarsi, Lamine Yamal and Dani Olmo.

news.title: Who Are Spain’s Best Players in the 2026 World Cup Final?
news.lede: Spain is back in the FIFA World Cup Final for the first time since 2020, when they defeated the Netherlands 1-0 on an extra-time goal in the 116th minute by Andres Iniesta. That Spain team featured such now-legendary stars as Davis Villa, Fernando Torres, Cesc Fabregas, David Silva and more. So who are the best players for Spain 16 years four World Cup tournaments later? This year’s team combines established international stars with a new generation of elite talent.

news.title: Stat: Lionel Messi & Lamine Yamal make history with World Cup final starts
news.lede: Barcelona stars past and present have on Sunday come together to make history on the international football stage. This comes amid confirmation of both Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal being afforded expected starting berths for this evening’s World Cup final. The biggest fixture in world football is of course set to be played out in short order. After putting France and England to the sword in their respective semi-final fixtures, Spain and Argentina will lock horns will the World Cup trophy on the line.

news.title: Are money and soft power draining World Cup football of its magic? | Richard Partington
news.lede: Fifa’s orchestration of incompatible or frankly absurd corporate sponsorship suggests the balance may have tipped too far. No more hydration breaks, no more obligatory pans of the TV cameras to Hollywood A-listers, or dread of the crushing inevitability of English disappointment. The Fifa World Cup 2026 is at an end, after another month of planet-straddling drama laced with significance and symbolism, both on and off the pitch. Before the kick-off between Spain and Argentina on Sunday evening, the result is already clear: football’s greatest prize is more than a sporting event; it is a geopolitically charged economic juggernaut.

news.title: What to know about the World Cup final's Super Bowl-style half-time show
news.lede: But the 11-minute performance is not without its critics, as the break between Argentina and Spain at New York New Jersey Stadium on Monday morning (AEST) is expected to stretch well beyond the 15-minute interval permitted under International Football Association Board laws. FIFA has bent the rules before. Last year's Club World Cup final featured a 25-minute half-time break to accommodate performances by J Balvin, Doja Cat, and Tems. The Times, citing broadcast sources, reported the World Cup final half-time break could last up to 30 minutes.
